suggested: What documents do I need to register?
Can I vote without a Voter ID?
How do I find my polling booth?

answer.documents: You will usually need three things: a recent passport-size photo, proof of age, and proof of address. Clear files help avoid rejection.
answer.deadline: Voter rolls usually close around 30 days before an election. Check your local schedule on the timeline and official ECI pages.
answer.vvpat: VVPAT is the paper trail attached to the EVM. After you vote, it briefly shows your chosen candidate before the slip drops into a sealed box.
answer.voterId: Besides the Voter ID, several ECI-approved photo IDs are also accepted at the booth.
answer.register: To register, fill Form 6 on the official voter portal and upload your supporting documents.
answer.booth: Use the official electoral search to find your assigned polling booth by name or voter ID.
answer.eepic: The e-EPIC is the digital version of your voter identity that you can download and store on your phone.
answer.age: You can vote if you meet the qualifying-date age rule for that election cycle.
answer.next: Your next step is "{{title}}". Open the step page to see what to do, which documents you need, and how long it should take.
answer.nextDone: You have completed every step — open the completion screen to review and share your journey.
answer.location: Your registered location is {{city}}. Polling booths are assigned based on your address, so use the booth-finder step to confirm yours.
answer.fallback: I do not have a precise answer for that yet. Try opening your next step or browse the Help page for verified guidance.
